business_description: Full-service nail salons offering manicures, pedicures, and related beauty services, primarily located inside Walmart stores

affiliates.name: Alfalfa Nail Supply, Inc.
affiliates.role: Exclusive supplier; owned by CEO Quy T. Ton

litigation_summary: 3 collection actions; 2 state agency actions (VA 2007 $21K settlement, CA 2005 desist order) for unregistered franchise sales

monthly_fee.includes: Franchise fee + base rent + rental tax if party tenant

royalty_rate: Bundled in Monthly Fee — not separately stated
percentage_rent_walmart: 6.5% of Gross Sales above natural breakpoint

exclusive_suppliers: Alfalfa Nail Supply (furniture, fixtures, equipment, branded products)

financing: No direct financing; indirect via sublease only

territory.encroachment_risk: High — franchisor may open in same shopping center

special_risks: Supplier control — must purchase from franchisor/affiliates at their prices
Financial condition — calls into question ability to provide services and support
Sales performance required — minimum payments regardless of sales levels

unresolveds.description: Financial condition risk: Special Risk #3 states franchisor's financial condition calls into question ability to provide services — yet auditor issued clean opinion with no going concern. CA DFPI and IL addendum both flag this concern separately.
unresolveds.source: Cover p.4, Exhibit E (CA/IL), Exhibit H

unresolveds.description: Walmart 90-day no-cause termination: 2009 MLA allows Walmart to terminate any individual location lease on 90 days notice without cause — existential risk not within franchisee's control.
Percentage-based rent transition: Walmart contemplating monthly rent based on % of gross sales (up to 20%) with minimum base. Could materially change economics.
Accelerating non-renewals: 9→16→21 over three years. Root cause unclear (Walmart, franchisee, performance covenant, or franchisor decisions).
Related party lending: $6.47M in notes to owner ($2.57M) and president ($3.90M) — 45% of total equity. 11 separate notes to president.
Alfalfa pricing independence: FDD claims no control over Alfalfa pricing while CEO sole-owns both entities.
Zero projected new outlets despite 14 signed-but-not-opened agreements.
Credit loss expense increased 130% to $432,977 (2024) from $188,132 (2023), suggesting deteriorating franchisee payment performance.

contradictions.description: Cover page Special Risk #3 states financial condition concerns vs. auditor's clean opinion with no going concern. CA DFPI fee deferral and IL addendum financial condition flag support the risk disclosure.
Item 1/8 claim franchisor does not control Alfalfa's pricing while CEO is sole owner of both entities.
